code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test24-MAR-2017Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The results indicate impaired renal function (elevated creatinine, proteinuria, low urine specific gravity), mild hypoglycemia, and metabolic acidosis (low CO₂, high chloride). Urinalysis shows proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), but no evidence of infection. Findings are suggestive of possible diabetic nephropathy or another chronic kidney disease; clinical correlation and further nephrological evaluation are recommended.

coding.display: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus

Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
